Glossary of Launch Terms
Launch Terms
Glossary
Below you’ll find common terms used throughout PLF and in the online marketing world.
Avatar:
Your avatar is a composite representation of your ideal client.
Close Cart: (or Cart Close)
The final period in your launch when you close your cart (redirect your webpage or
disable the “buy” button on your sales page) - and your offer closes to new clients.
Email Management System:
The platform that collects and manages all email lists, broadcasts, autoresponder
sequences and email opt-ins. Examples include Aweber, GetResponse, or Infusionsoft.
Evergreen:
Taking a one-time product launch and turning it into an ongoing offer. Usually done by
creating an autoresponder email sequence that goes to people after they’ve gotten on your
email list and after a specific period of time, it takes them through your launch email
sequence and a limited time Open Cart period.
Internal Launch:
Internal Launches are launches to just your list without the presence of affiliates or joint
venture partners.
JV Launch:
A Joint Venture Launch is a launch that includes the help of other individuals supporting
you and selling your product to people on their list.
Launch List:
The list people go on when opting into your Prelaunch Content during your Launch.
Lead Magnet/ Opt-in Offer/ “Freebie Offer”:
A small and valuable piece of information that provides instant value to your offered in
exchange for someone's email address.
Learning Management System (LMS):
A system to deliver course content, videos, audio files and pdf downloads to users.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
Glossary of Launch Terms
Niche:
A small but specific and well defined segment of a larger market created by identifying
needs, wants, and opportunities.
Offer:
The lead magnet, product, service, or training being offered (in exchange for an email
address in the case of your opt-in offer, or money in the case of your launch). It includes
the “stuff” they get, and the transformation that results in having that “stuff.”
Opt-in:
When someone gives you their email address in exchange for your opt-in offer (Lead
Magnet).
Open Cart:
The period of time immediately after Prelaunch (and starts with the release of your Sales
Video) when the product or program is open for orders -- generally 3-7 days.
PLF: (Product Launch Formula ;-) )
The formula used by thousands of entrepreneurs in hundreds of niches around the world
to more effectively market and sell what the market needs and wants (and generate well
over $500 million dollars in revenue for entrepreneurs like you).
PLC: (Prelaunch Content)
Your Prelaunch Sequence will generally have three pieces of Prelaunch Content (PLC).
Each piece of Prelaunch Content has a specific job and is often delivered via video but can
also be blog posts, podcast episodes, emails or other options (but video is most common).
PLC 4:
Some refer to the Sales Video as PLC 4, as it is the 4th video in the series, released with
Open Cart.
Prelaunch:
A period of 7-12 days leading up to ‘Open Cart’ which typically includes the Sideways
Sales Letter to build momentum and anticipation for the release of the product.
Pre-Prelaunch:
This is where you lay the groundwork for your launch, it typically lasts 1-4 weeks. During
this time, you deliver content, create inspiration identify objections, craft and refine your
offer, gather testimonials, build on mental triggers and start generating buzz for your
launch.
PS (Problem-Solution) Path:
A sequence where a problem is identified and then a solution is provided. Upon
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 3 --

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
Glossary of Launch Terms
providing the solution to the first problem, a second problem is identified, providing an
opportunity to share another solution.
Seed Launch:
A type of launch usually characterized as having only 1 PLC video followed immediately
by a Sales Video. Often used when people have no list and no product developed in the
online market. It’s how you get paid before you create your product.
Shot Across the Bow:
Part of the Pre-Prelaunch and delivered in the form of a survey used to the used to
identify objections and gather information and interest.
Sideways Sales Letter:
This takes the traditional long form sales letter and breaks it into 3 pieces of content. The
three pieces include “Opportunity”, “Transformation” and the “Ownership Experience.”
Also known as Prelaunch Content (PLC).
Squeeze Page/ Landing Page/ Opt-in page:
A one-page website where customers can provide their name and email in exchange for a
Lead Magnet/ “Freebie”.
Quick Launch:
Generally done all via email, use this if you already have a list. Create a special offer, such
as discount or bundle price, an intriguing story, and with a short Open Cart, it’s all done
within 2-5 days.
What else do you think should be on this list? Add your glossary term to the comments
below this download in the portal.
